name: Schisandra Extract
summary: Human evidence is insufficient for stress, liver, or performance authority claims without verified clinical citation.
description: Schisandra extracts show plausible bioactivity for stress and hepatic pathways, but human evidence quality is insufficient to support authoritative claims without citations.
safetyNotes: Possible drug interactions through hepatic enzyme effects; caution hepatically metabolized drugs.
contraindications: pregnancy due to traditional uterine-stimulant and antifertility use in Chinese medicine
active acute infection or excess-heat conditions per traditional-use restrictions
tacrolimus or cyclosporine due to potent CYP3A4 inhibition that markedly raises blood levels of these immunosuppressants
statins or calcium channel blockers or benzodiazepines due to CYP3A4-mediated increases in drug exposure
active peptic ulcer or gastroesophageal reflux due to its astringent acid-stimulating action
